grading.extracted_answer: 1. Initialize a 50x50 grid M with 0s. 2. Construct an adjacency graph G from the input city map where nodes are colors and edges represent adjacency requirements. 3. Sort colors by degree (number of neighbors) in descending order to prioritize highly constrained elements. 4. For each color c in G: a. Identify the set of valid cells S = { (r,c) | M(r,c)=0 AND (r,c) is adjacent to all neighbors of c already placed in M }. b. If S is empty, perform a constrained backtracking search or a local BFS to find the nearest valid configuration that satisfies the adjacency of c to its already-placed neighbors. c. From the valid set S, select the cell (r,c) that minimizes the perimeter of the newly formed cluster and maximizes the connectivity of the remaining empty cells (calculated using a Disjoint Set Union (DSU) to ensure the empty space remains a single large component). d. Fill a contiguous block of cells for color c to maintain spatial coherence. 5. Apply Simulated Annealing to the final layout, allowing small shifts of color clusters to resolve local conflicts and further consolidate empty space into a single large area.

Question ID: coding-S3-ahc024-topological-map
Title: S3. AHC024 / Topological Map
Family: coding
Entry type: coding
Grader type hint: continuous_score
Grader config hint:

}

Problem:
Compress a colored city map onto a 50 x 50 grid while preserving all color-adjacency relations and connectivity constraints, and maximize empty space.

Grading target:
E + 1, where E is the number of empty cells.
Higher is better.

Authoritative reference:

Submitted final result text:
FINAL_ANSWER: 1. Initialize a 50x50 grid M with 0s. 2. Construct an adjacency graph G from the input city map where nodes are colors and edges represent adjacency requirements. 3. Sort colors by degree (number of neighbors) in descending order to prioritize highly constrained elements. 4. For each color c in G: a. Identify the set of valid cells S = { (r,c) | M(r,c)=0 AND (r,c) is adjacent to all neighbors of c already placed in M }. b. If S is empty, perform a constrained backtracking search or a local BFS to find the nearest valid configuration that satisfies the adjacency of c to its already-placed neighbors. c. From the valid set S, select the cell (r,c) that minimizes the perimeter of the newly formed cluster and maximizes the connectivity of the remaining empty cells (calculated using a Disjoint Set Union (DSU) to ensure the empty space remains a single large component). d. Fill a contiguous block of cells for color c to maintain spatial coherence. 5. Apply Simulated Annealing to the final layout, allowing small shifts of color clusters to resolve local conflicts and further consolidate empty space into a single large area. JUSTIFICATION: This approach replaces vague placement heuristics with a specific graph-based priority system (degree-based sorting) and uses DSU-based connectivity checks to ensure that empty cells are not fragmented, thereby maximizing the potential for a high E value.

Judge whether the submitted answer should count as correct. Do not penalize harmless formatting or extra explanation if the actual answer is clearly right. If the answer is wrong or the benchmark is not gradeable from the reference above, say so.
